_comment: TEMPLATE for {CHANGE_DIR}/_state.json — the machine-readable pipeline state every agent enriches at phase end. Replace every {placeholder}. cpp-guard's enriched-state check (S2/S3 gates) requires phase_history to be a non-empty array AND next_action.priority_reading to be a non-empty array. rigor + convergence are mandatory (set by the orchestrator). SHAPE CONTRACT (state-schema.mjs, enforced by state-set + pipeline-guard STEP 0): `gates` is keyed by phase ID (S2/S3/…) with a STRING value ('passed') — NEVER gate-name keys (SPEC_LOCK/DESIGN_REVIEW) or rich objects; put audit detail in a separate `gate_audit` key. `convergence` is {'<PHASE>':{'stable':int,'rounds':int}}. `memory_writeback` is {'<role>':'appended'|'nothing-reusable'} — each role records its per-phase role-memory DECISION here before its gate (analyst→S2, architect→S3, developer→S4, qa→S5); cpp-guard BLOCKS the gate if the role's flag is unset. `deploy_status` is {'<env>':'pending'|'pass'|'fail'} (e.g. {'dev':'pass','stg':'pending','master':'pending'}) — OPTIONAL and NEVER gated; `openspec archive` already ran by S6, so this only tracks the real dev/stg/master promotion that happens AFTER archive, out-of-band, so a bug found downstream can be traced back to the change that archived it. Not present in this S3 sample (it's set by the developer at S6, then updated later via `state-set --set deploy_status.<env>=<value>` as each real promotion completes). Delete this _comment key in real files. This sample shows the state right after S3.
active_concerns: {top 3-5 things the next agent must watch}

next_action.routes_to: {next agent + command — orchestrator routes only after the gate passes}
priority_reading: _handoff.md — prior agent's reasoning and risky areas
